chief_complaint: A 72-year-old woman presented with fever, rash, eosinophilia, and liver dysfunction.
history: The patient initially presented with fever, rash, eosinophilia, and liver dysfunction following antibiotic exposure. She developed progressive cytopenias and neurological symptoms despite corticosteroid therapy.
exam: Vital signs: fever, tachycardia, and tachypnea. Physical examination revealed a rash and signs of neurological dysfunction.
labs: Laboratory investigations revealed eosinophilia, liver dysfunction, cytopenias, and evidence of hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis (HLH) and cryptococcal infection.
correct_diagnosis: Severe DRESS syndrome with hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis and cryptococcal meningitis
differentials: Severe DRESS syndrome with hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis and cryptococcal meningitis
correct_ranking: Severe DRESS syndrome with hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis and cryptococcal meningitis
